title: ranunculus indivisus
intro: Ranunculus indivisus, also known as Butcher's Broom, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Ranunculus indivisus include its ability to improve circulation, reduce swelling, and alleviate symptoms associated with varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and other circulatory issues. Ranunculus indivisus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
